title: tmuxcc wire protocol schema index
description:
Index of all JSON Schema files in the tmuxcc protocol package.

VERSION: 3 (WIRE_PROTOCOL_VERSION = 3)

Schema files:
  shared/primitives.json     — PaneId, WindowId, SessionId, MessageBase, Capabilities, WireFeature, ClientIdentity, ClientFlags
  shared/layout.json         — Rect, LayoutPane, LayoutHSplit, LayoutVSplit, LayoutNode, WindowLayout
  shared/session-template.json — SessionTemplate, WindowTemplate, TemplateNode desired-geometry tree, TemplateApplyResult
  session-proxy/server-push.json — All session-proxy→client messages (SessionProxyMessage union)
  session-proxy/client.json  — All client→session-proxy messages (ClientMessage union + WireCommand)
  server-proxy/server-push.json  — All server-proxy→client messages (ServerProxyMessage union)
  server-proxy/client.json   — All client→server-proxy messages (ServerProxyCommand union)
  data-plane-framing.json    — Binary frame layout documentation + DataFrame JSON representation

Amendment history:
  tc-gjdx.1 (additive — no version bump): new shared/session-template.json (SessionTemplate +
    desired-geometry tree). server-proxy/client.json: optional `template` on session.claim /
    session.create / session.createUnique; new session.applyTemplate + session.freezeTemplate
    commands. server-proxy/server-push.json: `applyTemplate` + `frozenTemplate` ok-payload fields;
    `template.invalid` error code. No layout strings / substitution syntax in the schema.
  tc-76m8.28 (additive — no version bump): ClientFlags.pullHydration in shared/primitives.json —
    client-declared pull-hydration: the session-proxy skips the unsolicited addClient bulk replay
    for clients that set it (they hydrate via pane.attach).
  tc-4b6k.1 (D2, additive — no version bump): ClientIdentity + ClientFlags $defs in
    shared/primitives.json; optional `identity` on ClientCapabilitiesMessage (both wires);
    optional info.clients[] in both *.info payloads. ClientFlags is a reserved slot for
    session.attach (tc-4b6k.4) — typed, not yet carried. See PROTOCOL.md §3.3 + §12.
  v3 (tc-j9c): ServerProxy wire introduced; session-proxy wire becomes single-session.
    SnapshotMessage: plural sessions[] → singular session.
    PaneOpenedMessage/PaneClosedMessage/etc.: sessionId stripped (bound session implicit).
    SessionRenamedMessage renamed to SessionProxySessionRenamedMessage; sessionId dropped.
    SessionClosedMessage removed; destruction surfaces as ErrorMessage{code:"session.unavailable"}.
    CommandRequestMessage/ResponseMessage/OkPayload renamed with SessionProxy prefix.
    OpenWindowCommand: sessionId dropped.

Amendability guidance for future beads:
  - W2.1 (verbs-with-effects): Add new WireCommand variants to session-proxy/client.json
    $defs/WireCommand oneOf array. Add corresponding new $def entries.
  - W3.1 (causality tags): Add a `causeId` or similar field to MessageBase in
    shared/primitives.json. Document the sequencing invariants in protocol/PROTOCOL.md.
  - W1.3 (SessionEntry enrichment): Amend ServerProxySessionInfo in
    server-proxy/server-push.json to add tmuxccMarked, paneCount, lastActivity.
    These fields are NOT present in this version.

Conformance:
  Implementations MUST validate against these schemas (in CI: packages/session-proxy
  uses ajv via src/wire/protocol-conformance.test.ts).
  Golden transcripts live in protocol/golden/.

protocolVersion.description: WIRE_PROTOCOL_VERSION. Monotonically-increasing integer identifying this schema revision.

schemas.description: PaneId, WindowId, SessionId, MessageBase, Capabilities, WireFeature
SessionTemplate, WindowTemplate, TemplateNode desired-geometry tree, TemplateApplyResult (tc-gjdx)
All client→session-proxy messages and WireCommand variants
All client→server-proxy messages and ServerProxyCommand variants
